1. Document Control
   Purpose: To establish the foundational metadata for the document, ensuring traceability and accountability.
   Expected Content: Document title, versioning details, approval authority, and revision history.
   Sample Elements:
   1.1 Document Title: Master and Reference Data Principles Document
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author

2. Executive Summary
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance within the organization.
   Expected Content: Summary of the purpose, scope, and key outcomes of the document.
   Sample Elements: This document outlines the foundational principles for master and reference data, establishing governance expectations and roles to enhance data quality and consistency across the organization.

3. Domain and Context Framing
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextualize the governance framework within the organization.
   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and current maturity positioning.
   Sample Elements:
   3.1 Domain Definition: Reference Master Data (RMD)
   3.2 Organizational Context: The principles apply to all business units managing master and reference data.
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 0 - Non-existent

4. Action Definition
   Purpose: To clearly articulate the action being defined, including its objectives and scope.
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objectives, and scope boundaries.
   Sample Elements:
   4.1 Action Code and Title: RMD-01 - Define foundational master and reference data principles and scope
   4.2 Action Objective: Establish foundational principles for master and reference data governance.
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Applies to all identified master and reference data domains within the organization.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary to achieve the defined action.
   Expected Content: Objectives, prerequisites, deliverables, and a detailed step-by-step execution structure.
   Sample Elements:
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To define and document master and reference data principles.
   5.2 Prerequisites: Identification of enterprise master and reference domains.
   5.3 Deliverables: Master and reference data principles document.
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:
   - Identify Master and Reference Data Domains
   - Draft Master and Reference Data Principles
   - Clarify Distinction Between Master and Reference Data
   - Align Principles with Governance Expectations
   - Validate Lifecycle Coverage
   - Document Principles Using DAMA Standards
   - Review and Approve Principles Document
   - Communicate and Train on Principles

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To define the governance framework that supports the execution of the action.
   Expected Content: Coverage of organizational and data assets, governance themes, structure, decision authority, processes, interfaces, and exclusions.
   Sample Elements:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All business units involved in data management.
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All master and reference data assets.
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, stewardship, ownership, and compliance.
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office.
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Data Owner for each data domain.
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Conduct workshops for domain identification.
   - Draft principles based on stakeholder input.
   - Review principles with Data Governance Council.
   - Communicate principles organization-wide.
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Integration with Data Quality and Data Architecture governance.
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Excludes operational data not classified as master or reference data.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms
   Purpose: To define the formal control and oversight mechanisms applicable to this action.
   Expected Content: Basic governance oversight definition aligned with foundational maturity.
8. Document Governance
   Purpose: To outline the management and review processes for the document itself.
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and document ownership.
   Sample Elements:
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually.
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by the Data Governance Council.
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office.

9. Appendices
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials that support the main document.
   Expected Content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.
   Sample Elements:
   9.1 Templates: Template for master and reference data principles.
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: List of stakeholders involved in the identification process.

   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, data stewardship, data ownership.
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Hierarchical representation of governance roles.

   - Implement data quality checks
   - Conduct regular data reviews
   - Facilitate stakeholder workshops
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Interaction with other governance domains.
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Areas not covered by this governance framework.
